overview.about: Mount Fuji, Japan's highest peak at 3,776m, is an active stratovolcano last erupted in 1707. It is a UNESCO World Heritage site revered as a spiritual symbol of Japan for over a millennium. Its near-perfect symmetrical cone has inspired countless Japanese artists including Hokusai and Hiroshige.

highlights.label: Japan's Sacred Peak — 3,776m
highlights.desc: An active stratovolcano and the spiritual heart of Japan — pilgrims have climbed its slopes for over 1,400 years

highlights.label: Hokusai's Eternal Muse
highlights.desc: Inspired Hokusai's iconic Thirty-six Views — the most reproduced image of any single mountain on Earth

highlights.label: 2-Hour Summit Climb
highlights.desc: Four official trails lead to the summit — the Yoshida trail is most popular with mountain huts at every station

highlights.label: Snow-Capped 9 Months of the Year
highlights.desc: Fuji's iconic white cap disappears only briefly in late summer — September sees bare summit and unique mist inversions

overview.about: Fushimi Inari Taisha in Kyoto is Japan's most important Shinto shrine dedicated to Inari, the deity of foxes, rice and prosperity. Its thousands of vermilion torii gates wind for 4km up the sacred Mount Inari. The shrine's main structures date to 711 AD, predating Kyoto's founding as the imperial capital.

highlights.label: 10,000 Vermilion Torii Gates
highlights.desc: Thousands of brilliant orange-red gates donated by businesses since 711 AD — forming a surreal tunnel for 4km

highlights.label: Dawn Photography — No Crowds
highlights.desc: Arrive before 6 AM to walk the entire gate tunnel in ethereal light with no other visitors around you

highlights.label: Founded 711 AD — 1,300 Years Old
highlights.desc: Predates Kyoto itself by 83 years — the foxes (kitsune) guarding the gates are messengers of the god Inari

highlights.label: 4km Trail to Mountain Summit
highlights.desc: Full circuit to the 233m summit takes 2–3 hours — city views from the top are an underrated Kyoto highlight

overview.about: Kiyomizu-dera in Kyoto, JP is a quintessential expression of Japanese heritage — where centuries of Shinto spirituality, Zen aesthetics and meticulous craftsmanship converge. As one of Japan's most celebrated heritage landmark sites, it offers an experience that is uniquely and profoundly Japanese.

highlights.label: Centuries of Living History
highlights.desc: Structures standing for hundreds to thousands of years — each stone a silent chronicle of empires and civilisations

highlights.label: UNESCO World Heritage Site
highlights.desc: Recognised globally for outstanding universal value and protected as one of Earth's most significant cultural landmarks

highlights.label: Iconic Photography Spot
highlights.desc: Panoramic vistas, intricate carvings and sunrise silhouettes that appear on bucket lists worldwide — every angle tells a story

highlights.label: Architectural Masterpiece
highlights.desc: Precision engineering predating modern tools — symmetry, scale and craftsmanship that continues to baffle experts today

highlights.label: Legendary Cuisine & Markets
highlights.desc: A melting pot of culinary traditions — street food, fine dining and bustling markets all within easy reach

highlights.label: Vibrant Nightlife & Events
highlights.desc: Rooftop bars, jazz clubs, night markets and international festivals keep the city electric long after dark

overview.about: Beautiful Honshu Luxury Resort in Honshu, JP is a quintessential expression of Japanese heritage — where centuries of Shinto spirituality, Zen aesthetics and meticulous craftsmanship converge. As one of Japan's most celebrated destination sites, it offers an experience that is uniquely and profoundly Japanese.

highlights.label: Centuries of Living History
highlights.desc: Structures standing for hundreds to thousands of years — each stone a silent chronicle of empires and civilisations

highlights.label: UNESCO World Heritage Site
highlights.desc: Recognised globally for outstanding universal value and protected as one of Earth's most significant cultural landmarks

highlights.label: Iconic Photography Spot
highlights.desc: Panoramic vistas, intricate carvings and sunrise silhouettes that appear on bucket lists worldwide — every angle tells a story

highlights.label: Architectural Masterpiece
highlights.desc: Precision engineering predating modern tools — symmetry, scale and craftsmanship that continues to baffle experts today

overview.about: Central Tokyo Mountain Range in Tokyo, JP is a quintessential expression of Japanese heritage — where centuries of Shinto spirituality, Zen aesthetics and meticulous craftsmanship converge. As one of Japan's most celebrated mountain destination sites, it offers an experience that is uniquely and profoundly Japanese.

highlights.label: Awe-Inspiring Peaks
highlights.desc: Stand beneath peaks that pierce the clouds — some of the highest, most dramatic mountain ranges on the planet

highlights.label: World-Class Trekking Routes
highlights.desc: Legendary trails traversing alpine meadows, glacial passes and ancient shepherd paths used for centuries

highlights.label: Snow Adventures
highlights.desc: Skiing, snowboarding and ice-trekking on slopes that remain covered well into spring and summer months

highlights.label: Sunrise Panoramas
highlights.desc: Golden hour from high-altitude viewpoints paints surrounding valleys in colours no photograph fully captures
